title: blechnum capense var. procerum
intro: Blechnum capense var. procerum, also known as the 'Tree Fern', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Blechnaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Blechnum capense var. procerum include relieving congestion, soothing sore throats, and even aiding in the treatment of asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, with the leaves and roots being used for their medicinal value.
